page.title: Compare
page.description: Compare geographic outputs from different runs or external sources on synchronized maps.

entry.noMatch: This lens could not be matched to a stored output

toast.unknownSource: A shared source could not be found and was removed from the link
toast.invalidLink: The comparison link contained an invalid source reference

basket.title: Sources
basket.setActive: Click to compare as source B

picker.title: Add comparison source
picker.recentRunsLimit: Showing the most recent runs only
picker.empty: No runs with stored geographic outputs yet. Run a forecast with a GRIB sink, or add external data below.

wmsUrl.title: External WMS server
wmsUrl.description: Any WMS endpoint — another FIAB instance, GeoServer, a met-service WMS. The server must allow cross-origin requests (CORS).
wmsUrl.errorUnreachable: Server not reachable — check the URL, and that it allows cross-origin (CORS) requests from this app
wmsUrl.errorHttp: The server responded with HTTP {{status}} — check the URL path and any token
wmsUrl.errorParse: The server responded, but not with a readable WMS capabilities document

hostPath.title: GRIB directory on this host
hostPath.description: A folder of GRIB files on the machine FIAB runs on — e.g. data generated by another system. A lens server is started on it directly.

lens.dirError: Could not resolve the output directory
lens.stopAllHint: Stops the lens servers behind the current sources. They are never stopped automatically.
lens.paused: Auto-start paused — start lenses per panel

panel.emptySlot: Pick a second source to compare
panel.pickPrompt: Click a source chip above, or add a new source
panel.corsHint: If this is an external server, it must send CORS headers

modes.flickerHint: Click the map or press Space to flip between A and B

timeline.label: Valid time
timeline.trackHint: Click to jump to a time step
timeline.clipAria: Clip the timeline to a window
timeline.clipBothHint: Clip to where both sources have data

link.autoUnlinked: The two sources share no common layers — selection is per panel.

browser.bothHint: Only layers available in both sources
browser.bothEmpty: No layers are available in both sources.

sidebar.empty: Add layers from the browser on the right.

export.title: Export comparison
export.description: Download the current view as PNG — title, valid time, and legends are baked in.
export.failed: Could not capture the map view

overlays.title: Context overlays
overlays.invalid: Could not read that file as GeoJSON

help.title: Compare viewer
help.intro: Two sources — A (blue) and B (orange) — are shown on synchronized maps. Pick sources in the header, choose layers on the right, and control what is on the map from the left.

modes.title: Comparison modes
modes.body: Swipe splits one map with a draggable divider (vertical or horizontal). Side by side shows two maps with a shared camera and a mirrored crosshair. Flicker swaps A/B in place (Space or click). Spy reveals B through a movable lens — shape and size are in the action row. Blend draws B over A with an adjustable weight.

layers.title: Layers & opacity
layers.body: The right sidebar lists what each source offers — search, filter by availability (All | A | B | A∩B), or narrow pressure levels. Linked mode adds the same parameter to both sides at once; unlink to pick per side. The left sidebar holds active layers with legends and a three-tier opacity: global × per-source × per-layer.

time.title: Valid time
time.body: The timeline covers the union of both sources; the colored tracks show where each has data (hover for the instant, click to jump). The link mode decides how sides follow the axis: exact (identical instants or hidden), nearest (snap within tolerance, offset tagged on the panel), fixed offset (B lags A by Δ), or independent axes.

tools.title: Tools
tools.body: Measure distance or area with the ruler tools (results stay until cleared). Hold Z for a magnifier loupe. The annotate tool (N) records numbered findings pinned to the map — per panel in side-by-side — and they are baked into exports. The layers icon switches the basemap and its opacity. Upload GeoJSON in the left sidebar to draw boundaries or tracks over every panel.

export.title: Export
export.body: The download icon captures the current view as PNG — with the title, valid time, and the active layers’ legends baked in. Side by side saves one image per panel.

shortcuts.title: Keyboard shortcuts
shortcuts.revealHint: Hold {{key}} to see shortcut badges on the toolbar.

annotations.title: Annotations
annotations.tool: Annotate — click the map to add a note
annotations.empty: Arm the annotate tool and click the map to record findings.
annotations.armedHint: Click the map to add a note · Esc exits

emptyB.title: Choose what to compare against
emptyB.selfHint: Same source on both sides — compare two time steps of one forecast. Then pick “Time offset” or “Independent times” in the timeline.
